name: Skill Challenge: Beat 4a \u2014 The Coastal Road

text.content:
Skill Challenge: Beat 4a \u2014 The Coastal Road

Act: Act 1 | Difficulty: Hard (DC 15)

Successes Needed: 6 | Failures Allowed: 2

Overview

The party has left Hrafnborg before dawn with forged papers and the King's supplies. Ahead: the Lantern Road north through frozen farmland, the treacherous Frostfen Marshes, and the refugee-choked Herdweg junction — roughly 100 miles and 4–5 days on failing roads through the Fimbulwinter.

This montage test covers the first leg of the journey to Stairwatch: from the city gates to the edge of Midgard's Shield foothills, before the Chainwarden Toll at Greypass.

Running the Skill Challenge

Each character takes a turn attempting one skill check. On their turn, a player describes how they're helping the party and rolls the appropriate skill against DC 15. A success adds to the group's total; a failure adds to the failure count. If the party reaches 6 successes before 2 failures, they succeed overall.

Suggested Skills:

  • Athletics
  • Acrobatics or Stealth
  • Arcana or Investigation
  • Perception, Survival, or Insight
  • Persuasion, Intimidation, or Deception

Players may also suggest creative skill uses — reward good roleplaying with advantage or a lower DC.

Complications

Round 1: The Lantern Road and the Frostfen

The road is failing. Mile-markers are buried or stolen. Chainwarden patrols demand papers from northbound travelers. Ahead, the Frostfen Marshes: thin ice over black water, a half-collapsed causeway, and a cold that feels wrong — wetter, heavier, like the land itself is exhaling.

  • Navigate the Lantern Road (Arcana or Investigation, DC 15) — Read buried markers, plot detours around collapsed sections, choose the right fork when the road splits.
  • Endure the cold and ration supplies (Athletics, DC 15) — Resist frostbite, carry gear through snowdrifts, ford frozen streams. Supplies must last ten days.
  • Deceive early Chainwarden patrols (Persuasion, Intimidation, or Deception, DC 15) — Talk past a roving patrol with forged papers; explain why you're heading north when everyone else goes south.
  • Scout safe passage through the Frostfen (Perception, Survival, or Insight, DC 15) — Find the old causeway stones buried under frost. Avoid thin ice hiding black water beneath.
  • Navigate the Frostfen at night (Acrobatics or Stealth, DC 15) — Cross the worst section after dark on ice-slick stones. Balance, leap tussocks, avoid the thin spots.
  • Read weather signs to avoid a squall (Perception, Survival, or Insight, DC 15) — A Fimbulwinter squall drops visibility to arm's length in minutes. Read the cloud-patterns and wind-shift before it hits.

Later Rounds

Round 2: The Frostfen Margins and the Refugee Road

Beyond the worst of the marsh, the road meets the Herdweg — the old drovers' highway running east from the Interior Belt. The junction is choked with refugees fleeing south toward Hrafnborg. The party is heading north. Questions will be asked.

  • Find shelter in the Frostfen margins (Arcana or Investigation, DC 15) — The flat marshland offers no natural shelter. Locate an old peat-cutter's hut, an overturned barge, or reed-banks thick enough to break the wind.
  • Cross the Herdweg junction without attention (Presence or Agility) — Push north through southbound refugee flow without drawing questions. Why are healthy travelers heading toward the danger?
  • Resist marsh-sickness (Athletics, DC 15) — The Frostfen's air carries something foul — not disease, but a heaviness in the lungs. A failed character wakes the next morning with a rattling cough (−1 to Might tests until a warm long rest).
  • Avoid refugee columns (Presence or Agility) — Avoid drawing attention from desperate column families who may turn dangerous if they think you're hoarding supplies.
  • Calm a panicked pack-mule (Persuasion, Intimidation, or Deception, DC 15) — The mule balks at a frozen corpse on the road and threatens to bolt with half the supplies.
  • Keep morale up at camp (Persuasion, Intimidation, or Deception, DC 15) — Four days of grey-white emptiness and freezing wind. Keep each other from despair with stories, songs, small comforts.

Outcomes

Interlude: The Extinguished Shrine

By the roadside, a cairn of stacked stones stands waist-high. Its centre is hollow — a bowl where a flame should burn. The ash is cold. Dead wildflowers, frost-brittle, lie scattered around the base. The keystone is carved with a single runic word: Remember.

If any PC makes an offering (even just a breath warmed and placed in the basin), the keystone warms for just a moment, then goes cold again. Yggdrasil is listening — but too weak to answer.

No mechanical effect. This is a moment to feel the weight of what is failing.

Interlude: The Drowned Village

The road crosses what was once a shallow lake. Rooftops jut from the ice at the lake's centre. A market feet (5 ft.), a temple spire, streets and doorways — and movement below. Something with hands.

The PCs should not investigate. If they do: the ice creaks, and whatever is below tracks their movement. An easy Reason test identifies this as a draugr nest — the village died and the residents didn't leave.

If they move on: they gain a grim truth. The dead are everywhere. The Frostfen is a cemetery that doesn't know it's dead.

Total Success (5+ successes): 2 Milestones. Arrive at the Herdweg junction intact, supplies holding, morale manageable. Delay first Grafvitnir Pressure Check by one scene.

Partial Success (3–4 successes): 1 Victory. Arrive at cost — choose one: (1) −1 day's rations lost navigating Frostfen; (2) one PC has persistent cough (−1 Might tests until warm long rest); or (3) a refugee recognised the King's seal on the papers (+1 to future Grafvitnir Pressure Checks).

Total Failure (2 failures before 5 successes): 0 Milestones. Arrive compromised: lose 2 days' rations, one PC has marsh-sickness, Grafvitnir agents know exact position (next Pressure Check triggers automatically at 5+). Director gains 3 Malice for the next encounter.
